$comment: Config for THIS repo's own self-check run only (.github/workflows/self-check.yml). The actions-pinned-gate's source and tests necessarily contain the version-comment markers they enforce (`# vX.Y.Z` after a pinned SHA), exactly like the source-leak scanner's own rule definitions contain the markers it detects. The skills-drift-gate files (source, test, and reusable workflow) carry the literal gate name `skills-drift-gate`, which the source-leak SLG_PLANNING_DOC rule treats as a `drift-gate` planning-artifact marker — a self-referential false positive identical in kind to the actions-pinned-gate case. Its release-closeout companion source + workflow (skills-drift-closeout-sweep.mjs, skills-drift-closeout-sweep.yml) carry the same literal `skills-drift-gate` name — the .mjs has a real `import ... from "./skills-drift-gate.mjs"` and the .yml names the companion gate in its header prose — so they are exempted on the same basis; that name cannot be rephrased away. The push-arm ack-collector's regression test (collect-skills-acks.test.mjs, eng#212) is exempted on the identical basis: it carries one IRREDUCIBLE real filename reference — it imports the gate at `../skills-drift-gate.mjs` — that cannot be rephrased; all rephraseable prose mentions were removed. The collector script itself (collect-skills-acks.sh) is NOT exempted — its only mention was prose that was reworded to drop the filename, so it stays fully scanned. The closeout sweep's TEST file is NOT exempted (it has no irreducible self-reference — its review/version markers were rephrased) so it stays fully scanned. All such files — plus self-check.yml itself, which lists the skills-drift gate test paths — are exempted here by basename; consuming repos never receive this config and are unaffected.
